summary: High‑energy 6/26/84 show with a rare Casey Jones opener and scorching Althea/China>Rider, captured on a soundboard cassette that shows noticeable hiss and generational loss.
review: Fans rave about the June 26, 1984 performance – the band is in top form, opening with the ultra‑rare Casey Jones and delivering fire‑hot versions of Althea, China>Rider, Stella and Brent’s “Don’t Need Love.”  The recording comes from a cassette‑master soundboard source, so while the music shines, listeners must contend with hiss, occasional drop‑outs and an extra generation that dulls clarity.  Overall the show’s brilliance outweighs its technical flaws.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (70%+) give 4‑5 stars and praise the performance, while recording quality is described as mixed (fair). Weighted combination yields ~4.1.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Cassette master with extra generations; noticeable hiss, occasional drop‑outs and muffled sections, but moments of crispness noted on tracks like “Don’t Need Love.”

show_quality.setlist_flow: Rare Casey Jones opener sets a high‑energy tone; first set stays tight and rock‑solid, second set builds into expansive jams (China>Rider, Stella) with strong interplay among the band.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for blistering guitar work, expressive phrasing and vocal power throughout the night.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for beautiful bass lines on “Looks Like Rain” and subtle contributions in the Space tease.
band_member_comments.Bob: Described as over‑the‑top and energetic, especially on “Good Lovin’”.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland highlighted for his soaring scream on “Don’t Need Love” and solid keyboard work throughout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Reviewers mention the drummers (Bill/ Mickey) finding a new groove in ’84, with tight interplay driving the jams.
